raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 276 will prohibit the use of a mobile device while driving for those aged 18-21 in California.",
    "Currently, motorists under the age of 18 in California are forbidden from using a mobile device while driving, regardless of whether it is operated in a hands-free capacity.",
    "The bill expands the age group of motorists prohibited from using their cell phone while driving.",
    "In 2021, a survey by the California Office of Traffic Safety discovered that 74.3% of Californians identified distracted driving as one of their biggest traffic safety concerns on California roadways.",
    "Data shows that distraction occurs significantly in drivers ages 15 through 20.",
    "Among drivers aged 15 through 20 involved in fatal crashes, 9% were distracted at the time of the crash.",
    "According to the Governor’s Highway Safety Association, teen drivers are 1.6 times more likely to be involved in a fatal crash than older drivers.",
    "The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention estimates that 30,000 fatalities occur annually in the United States because of a distracted driver.",
    "Distracted driving is a significant threat to road safety, and teen cell phone use is one of the biggest contributors to injuries and fatalities."
  ]
}
```
